channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Stephen Sanchez: From Church Drummer to Rising Music Star
item.description: Stephen Christopher Sanchez, born on 3 November 2002 in El Dorado Hills, California, is a young American singer and songwriter of Latino heritage. Raised in a bilingual Spanish-English household with ...

item.title: Photographer Jozef Macak Documents Life Along Dhaka’s Polluted Buriganga River
item.description: Slovak photographer Jozef Macak presents a compelling visual study of life along the Buriganga River in Dhaka, Bangladesh, through his project 'Tides of Life.' Known for its heavy pollution and nearin...

item.title: Treasurer Chalmers defends budget amid mixed media reception
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers is continuing to defend the 2026 federal budget as media outlets criticise its proposed tax changes. While the government promotes the budget as a bold response to calls for dec...

item.title: NPA Prosecutor Faces Corruption Allegations Over Bribe Linked to Fraud Case
item.description: Richard Chabalala, a senior prosecutor at the National Prosecuting Authority (NPA), is facing serious allegations of corruption. He is accused of soliciting a R400,000 bribe from Limpopo businessman H...

item.title: Trump delays planned military action on Iran after Gulf allies request more diplomatic time
item.description: President Donald Trump announced that he is postponing a planned military strike on Iran following appeals from Gulf allies, including Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the United Arab Emirates, urging more ti...

item.title: LTFRB Approves P10 Fuel Discount for Over 140,000 Jeepneys and UV Express Vehicles Nationwide
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has approved 141,724 units of passenger jeepneys and UV Express vehicles to benefit from the government's P10 per liter fuel discount. ...

item.title: South African Humanitarian Ambassador Faizel Moosa Detained During Gaza Aid Mission
item.description: South African humanitarian ambassador Faizel Moosa has been detained by Israeli authorities while participating in a civilian flotilla delivering humanitarian aid to Gaza. Moosa was part of the Global...

item.title: Analysis Highlights Inconsistencies in Comey Indictment Compared to Other Threat Cases
item.description: A national security journalist, Marcy Wheeler of Emptywheel, has identified notable inconsistencies in the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey, suggesting political motivations behind the ca...

item.title: JPC Chairman Says Political Consensus Required for Mid-Term Poll Cut-Off Under One Nation-One Election Plan
item.description: P.P. Choudhary, chairman of the Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) on simultaneous elections, stated that deciding the 'remainder of the term' for Lok Sabha or State Assemblies prior to elections wil...

item.title: Jim Cramer Comments on Trump's Extensive Stock Trading Amid Ethical Concerns
item.description: During a CNBC segment, financial analyst Jim Cramer appeared visibly confused while discussing President Donald Trump's recent stock trading activities. Government Ethics Office disclosures revealed t...

item.title: Transport Strike Paralyzes Nairobi Over Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: On Monday, Nairobi witnessed major commuter chaos as a nationwide transport strike left thousands stranded. Matatus, buses, truckers, boda bodas, digital taxis, and private motorists all joined the st...

item.title: Kelvin Wamae Switches from Engineering to Full-Time Pencil Art, Gains Local and International Recognition
item.description: Kelvin Wamae, a JKUAT-trained geospatial engineer, chose to leave a conventional engineering career to follow his lifelong passion for pencil art. Inspired by his late father to initially pursue engin...

item.title: FBI Seeks to Purchase Nationwide License Plate Data for Law Enforcement and Intelligence Purposes
item.description: The FBI has announced plans to spend up to $36 million to acquire nationwide access to automated license plate reader (ALPR) data. The agency aims to obtain detailed vehicle information—including lice...

item.title: Anderson Cooper Discusses Pardoned January 6 Rioter Linked to Fraudulent Restitution Claim
item.description: CNN's Anderson Cooper highlighted a surprising case involving a pardoned participant in the January 6 Capitol insurrection. Edward Kelly, convicted in 2024 for plotting to kill FBI agents who investig...

item.title: Canada Confirms One Hantavirus Case from Cruise Ship Outbreak
item.description: Canada's Public Health Agency confirmed that one Canadian who recently returned from a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for the virus. The case involves a person in th...

item.title: Modern Spacecraft Materials Increase Risk of Surviving Debris Reentering Earth's Atmosphere
item.description: The increasing use of heat-resistant materials in spacecraft is changing the way space debris behaves during reentry, raising safety concerns worldwide. Traditionally, satellites and rocket components...

item.title: No Jackpot Winners for May 18 PCSO Lotto Draws
item.description: On May 18, 2026, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) conducted its major lotto draws, but no bettor managed to win the jackpot prizes. For the Grand Lotto 6/55, the winning numbers were 4...

item.title: Federal High Court grants ₦5 million bail to influencer Justice Chidiebere over cybercrime allegations
item.description: Social media influencer Justice Chidiebere, popularly known as Justice Crack, don collect bail of ₦5 million from Federal High Court in Abuja. The bail na result of cybercrime and breach of public pea...

item.title: Curated Highlights of the Best Photobooks of 2025
item.description: LensCulture has compiled a comprehensive list of over 50 photobooks that stood out in 2025, curated by photography experts and enthusiasts worldwide. The collection spans a remarkable diversity of sty...

item.title: Patrick Radden Keefe Discusses London Case of Teenager Posing as Russian Billionaire
item.description: Investigative journalist Patrick Radden Keefe, in Sydney ahead of his Australian book tour, delves into the mysterious death of a teenager who masqueraded as a Russian billionaire. In his latest book,...
